# Visual Design Specifications

This directory contains the **Visual Reference Images** for the Metro Integrated Planning System.

> **Important:** These images are AI-generated and represent the VISION, not pixel-perfect specifications.
> They capture the essence of what we're building: a "Single Pane of Glass" with semantic zoom.

---

## Core Design Vision

### The "80% Cognitive Load Reduction" Goal

The system aims to reduce analysis and planning overhead by 80%+ through:

| Principle                   | Description                                | Implementation                  |
|-----------------------------|--------------------------------------------|---------------------------------|
| **Single Pane of Glass**    | Everything in one view, no page navigation | Pan/zoom instead of tabs        |
| **Semantic Zoom**           | Context changes with zoom level            | 3 LOD layers (Macro/Meso/Micro) |
| **Direct Manipulation**     | Drag to reschedule, not forms              | Future: drag-and-drop tasks     |
| **Game-like Visualization** | WebGL beyond standard charts               | R3F with custom shaders         |

### Design Inspirations

- **Google Earth/Maps:** Seamless zoom from planet to street
- **Strategy Games:** Information density that doesn't overwhelm (Factorio, Cities Skylines)
- **Financial Terminals:** Dense data, clear hierarchy (Bloomberg Terminal)

---

## Screen Reference Analysis

### screen-1.png: Simulation Setup

![Simulation Setup](screen-1.png)

**What it shows:**
- Maintenance Heatmap in background
- Modal: "Simulation Setup: Scenario A"
- Constraint sliders (Labor Availability: 50%)
- Date range selector
- Infrastructure toggle (Maintenance Pit Status)
- "Run Solver (Est. 45s)" button

**Design Intent:**
- What-If scenarios start with constraint adjustment
- Solver is async with time estimate
- Simple controls for complex simulations

**Implementation:** Future feature (Scenario Mode)

---

### screen-2.png: Full System with Zoom Popup

![Full System](screen-2.png)

**What it shows:**
- Header KPIs: "Fleet Avail: 98%", "Annual Budget: On Track"
- Maintenance Heatmap (2021-2022+)
- Legend: Routine maintenance, Mid-life overhauls, Capacity bottlenecks
- Zoom popup: "Zoom in 4 weeks" detail view
- Tasks with durations: "Brake Rev. T104 (8h)", "Wheel Lathe T112 (4h)"
- Dependency arrows between tasks
- Time Scrubber at bottom

**Design Intent:**
- Global KPIs always visible
- Click-to-zoom for detail exploration
- Dependencies are visual, not hidden in data

**Implementation:**
- Header: `apps/canvas/src/components/AppNavbar.tsx` (future)
- Zoom popup: Click handler вҶ’ animated zoom transition
- Time Scrubber: `apps/canvas/src/components/TimeScrubber.tsx` (future)

---

### screen-3.jpg: Multi-Scale View (All 3 LODs)

![Multi-Scale View](screen-3.jpg)

**What it shows:**

| LOD Level | Time Scale | Visualization              | Planning Horizon |
|-----------|------------|----------------------------|------------------|
| **Macro** | 12 years   | Heatmap (density clusters) | Strategic        |
| **Meso**  | 2 years    | DNA strips (task blocks)   | Tactical         |
| **Micro** | 4 weeks    | Detailed Gantt (cards)     | Operational      |

- Task tooltip: "Duration: 8h | Resources: 2 Techs, Pit 3 | Cost: $1200"
- Time Scrubber spanning all views

**Design Intent:**
- Same data, different aggregations per zoom level
- Each LOD answers different planning questions
- Seamless transitions (no loading states)

**Implementation:**
- MacroLayer: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MacroLayer.tsx`
- MesoLayer: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MesoLayer.tsx`
- MicroLayer: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MicroLayer.tsx`

---

### screen-4.png: Campaign View with Conflict Alert

![Campaign View](screen-4.png)

**What it shows:**
- All 3 LOD views stacked
- Complex dependency arrows in Micro view
- Task grouping: "Task Group: Bogie Overhaul Campaign | T145, T146, T147"
- Resource detail: "8 Techs, Pit 1 & 2, Lift Crane"
- **Conflict Alert:** "Overlapping with Signaling Upgrade"

**Design Intent:**
- Tasks can be grouped into "Campaigns" (related work)
- Conflicts are explicit alerts, not just visual indicators
- Resources include equipment (Lift Crane), not just man-hours

**Data Implications:**
- Need `Campaign` entity grouping related tasks
- Need `Equipment` dimension (Pit, Lift Crane, Wheel Lathe)
- Conflict detection must span multiple constraint types

---

### screen-5.png: Meso View with Capacity Chart

![Capacity Chart](screen-5.png)

**What it shows:**
- Train IDs (T001-T050) on Y axis
- 2028-2029 timeline on X axis
- Diagonal task pattern (staggered scheduling)
- **Capacity Utilization Chart at bottom:**
  - Mechanical work (blue bars)
  - Electrical work (yellow bars)
  - Capacity Limit line
  - Utilization over time

**Design Intent:**
- Diagonal pattern = "Equalizing" (Tokyo Metro methodology)
- Capacity split by work type, not just total
- Visual correlation between schedule and capacity

**Data Implications:**
- Tasks need `workType: "mechanical" | "electrical"` attribute
- `FactResourceUsage` must track by type
- Capacity chart component synced with main timeline

**Implementation:**
- Capacity Chart: `apps/canvas/src/components/CapacityChart.tsx` (future)

---

### screen-6.png: Micro View with Pit Occupancy

![Pit Occupancy](screen-6.png)

**What it shows:**
- Header: "Fleet Avail: 99%", "Conflict Alerts: 0"
- Zoom Level: Micro (4 Weeks)
- Train rows with tasks and dependencies
- **Pit Occupancy Panel:**
  - Pit 1, Pit 2, Pit 3, Wash Bay rows
  - Tasks shown in allocated resource
  - **Red conflict indicator** on overlapping pit assignment
- Time scale selector: 1 | 2 | 4 | 6 Weeks

**Design Intent:**
- Pit allocation is first-class visualization
- Conflicts visible at resource level
- Flexible time window for operational planning

**Data Implications:**
- Need `FactPitOccupancy` or `pitAssignment` on tasks
- Wash Bay as separate resource type
- Conflict detection at pit level

**Implementation:**
- Pit Occupancy: `apps/canvas/src/components/PitOccupancy.tsx` (future)

---

### screen-7.png: Scenario Diff View

![Scenario Diff](screen-7.png)

**What it shows:**
- Title: "Advanced Gantt - Diff View: Scenario Comparison"
- Conflict zone: Red hatched area (Oct 1-3)
- Original task position: Red border (in conflict)
- Resolved task position: Blue (moved to Oct 6)
- Arrow showing movement from conflict вҶ’ resolution
- Gray tasks: Unchanged in scenario

**Design Intent:**
- Visual diff more powerful than table diff
- Conflict zones shown as areas, not just task borders
- Resolution path is explicit (arrow shows "what changed")

**Color Coding:**
- Red hatched = Conflict zone
- Red border = Task in conflict
- Blue = Modified/resolved task
- Gray = Unchanged task

**Implementation:**
- Scenario comparison: `apps/canvas/src/components/ScenarioDiff.tsx` (future)

---

## Technical Requirements by Layer

### MacroLayer (zoom < 0.5x)

**Purpose:** Strategic 10-20 year overview

**Visual:** Heatmap with color gradient showing task density/health

**Technical:**
- Single `PlaneGeometry` covering visible area
- Fragment shader receiving `DataTexture`
- Color ramp: Yellow (low) вҶ’ Orange вҶ’ Red (high density)
- Target: 1 draw call

**Implementation:** `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MacroLayer.tsx`

---

### MesoLayer (0.5x вүӨ zoom вүӨ 2x)

**Purpose:** Tactical 2-5 year planning

**Visual:** DNA-like diagonal strips of task blocks

**Technical:**
- `InstancedMesh` with up to 60,000 instances
- Colors via `InstancedBufferAttribute`
- Spatial Hash Grid for hover detection
- Alternating row backgrounds

**Implementation:** `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MesoLayer.tsx`

---

### MicroLayer (zoom > 2x)

**Purpose:** Operational 4-8 week scheduling

**Visual:** Detailed task cards with labels, grid, dependencies

**Technical:**
- `<Html>` component from drei with `transform`
- `Line2` (Fat Lines) for dependency arrows
- BlueprintJS styling (Card, Tag, Intent)
- Accessibility: tabIndex, ARIA labels

**Implementation:** `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MicroLayer.tsx`

---

## Color Palette

### Task Colors

| Type | Color | Hex | Usage |
|------|-------|-----|-------|
| Routine/A-Check | Deep Blue | `#3b82f6` | Standard maintenance |
| Overhaul/D-Check | Orange | `#f59e0b` | Major overhauls |
| Inspection | Gray | `#6b7280` | Quick inspections |

### Semantic Colors

| Meaning | Color | Hex | BlueprintJS Intent |
|---------|-------|-----|-------------------|
| Conflict/Alert | Red | `#ef4444` | Intent.DANGER |
| Warning | Yellow | `#eab308` | Intent.WARNING |
| Success/Resolved | Green | `#22c55e` | Intent.SUCCESS |
| Neutral | Blue | `#3b82f6` | Intent.PRIMARY |

### UI Colors

| Element | Color | Hex |
|---------|-------|-----|
| Background | Soft gray | `#f4f4f5` (zinc-100) |
| Card background | White | `#ffffff` |
| Grid lines | Light gray | `#e5e7eb` |
| Dependency arrows | Slate | `#64748b` |

### Heatmap Gradient

```
Low density    вҶ’    Medium    вҶ’    High density
#fef3c7 (yellow)  #fb923c (orange)  #dc2626 (red)
```

---

## Design Principles

### 1. Progressive Disclosure via Zoom

Don't show everything at once. Zoom level determines information density.

| Zoom | Shows | Hides |
|------|-------|-------|
| Macro | Density patterns, hotspots | Individual tasks |
| Meso | Task blocks, patterns | Task details, labels |
| Micro | Full details, dependencies | Big picture |

### 2. Spatial Memory over Navigation

Users learn where things are. No page switches, just pan/zoom.

- Train T001 is always at the top
- 2025 is always to the left of 2026
- Context preserved during exploration

### 3. Conflicts are First-Class Citizens

Not hidden in tooltips or logs. Explicit visual treatment.

- Red indicators at task level
- Red hatched zones for conflict areas
- Alert badges in header
- Conflict explanation in tooltips

### 4. Resources are Visible

Pit occupancy shown, not just calculated internally.

- Capacity charts alongside schedule
- Equipment tracking (Wheel Lathe, Lift Crane)
- Utilization percentages in real-time

### 5. Scenarios are Comparable

Side-by-side or overlay comparison for What-If analysis.

- Diff view shows what changed
- Metrics delta (cost, conflicts, utilization)
- Visual path from problem вҶ’ solution

---

## Implementation Status

### Completed (POC Validated)

- [x] Semantic zoom concept (reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html)
- [x] 3 LOD layers with smooth transitions
- [x] Pan/zoom navigation
- [x] Sidebar sync with canvas
- [x] Timeline header with adaptive granularity
- [x] Conflict indicators (red highlighting)

### In Progress (Production App)

- [x] F101: Vite + React 18 + BlueprintJS skeleton
- [x] F102: R3F Canvas + OrthographicCamera
- [x] F103: BlueprintJS layout (Navbar, Card sidebar)
- [ ] F104: Data types and mock generator вҶҗ **NEXT**
- [ ] F105-F117: Remaining features

### Future Features (Post-Demo)

- [ ] Scenario comparison (diff view)
- [ ] Pit Occupancy panel
- [ ] Capacity utilization chart
- [ ] Campaign grouping
- [ ] Solver integration
- [ ] Drag-and-drop rescheduling

---

## Open Questions

1. **Dependency arrows at Meso level?** May clutter, but shows relationships
2. **Capacity chart position?** Bottom (like screen-5) or collapsible panel?
3. **Pit Occupancy visibility?** Always visible or toggle?
4. **Conflict resolution UX?** Solver suggests, human approves?

---

*Last updated: 2025-12-15*
*Source: AI-generated reference images representing design vision*

content.content:
1вҶ’# MMS Design Specification 2вҶ’ 3вҶ’> **Internal Document** - Implementation guide for the interactive demo with Vite + React 18 + deck.gl 4вҶ’> 5вҶ’> **Source of Truth**: `docs/RFQ.md` only 6вҶ’> 7вҶ’> **Primary Feature**: Planning View with Sandbox Mode (What-If Scenarios) 8вҶ’ 9вҶ’--- 10вҶ’ 11вҶ’## 1. Executive Summary 12вҶ’ 13вҶ’### 1.1 Objective 14вҶ’ 15вҶ’Win the bidding process by demonstrating an exceptional frontend solution that addresses all client pain points from the RFQ. The demo must visually impress while proving we can deliver a production-ready "Single Pane of Glass" for Rolling Stock Maintenance. 16вҶ’ 17вҶ’### 1.2 RFQ Pain Points вҶ’ Our Solutions 18вҶ’ 19вҶ’| # | Client Pain Point (RFQ) | Our Solution | Key Visual Element | 20вҶ’|---|------------------------|--------------|-------------------| 21вҶ’| 1 | No dedicated mileage management tools | Purpose-built mileage dashboard | KPI Cards + Mileage indicators | 22вҶ’| 2 | IBM Maximo only does asset management | Integrated mileage + maintenance planning | Unified Planning View | 23вҶ’| 3 | Manual Excel management | Interactive visual timeline with drag operations | Gantt-style canvas | 24вҶ’| 4 | Folder-driven data storage | Centralized fleet visualization | Sidebar + Fleet grid | 25вҶ’| 5 | Limited maintenance-operations integration | Single pane for both teams | Shared workspace | 26вҶ’| 6 | Non-compliance risk (underrun/overrun) | Visual alerts + compliance indicators | Color-coded tasks + banners | 27вҶ’| 7 | **No predictive simulations** | **Sandbox Mode for what-if scenarios** | **PRIMARY FEATURE** | 28вҶ’| 8 | Insufficient resource visibility | Resource utilization panels | Heatmap + capacity bars | 29вҶ’| 9 | Manual handling delays | Automated scheduling visualization | Smart conflict detection | 30вҶ’ 31вҶ’--- 32вҶ’ 33вҶ’## 2. Design Philosophy 34вҶ’ 35вҶ’### 2.1 Inspiration Attribution 36вҶ’ 37вҶ’We draw from multiple sources, each contributing specific elements: 38вҶ’ 39вҶ’| Source | What We Take | What We DON'T Take | 40вҶ’|--------|--------------|-------------------| 41вҶ’| **Linear App** | Dark mode aesthetic, typography, whitespace, micro-interactions | Sprint/issue tracking patterns (different domain) | 42вҶ’| **JetBrains Fleet Islands UI** | Modular layout, panel separation, information density | IDE-specific features | 43вҶ’| **DHTMLX Gantt** | Timeline visualization, task bars, dependency lines | Light theme default | 44вҶ’| **Railnova Railfleet** | Fleet dashboard patterns, train status indicators | Specific ECM workflows | 45вҶ’| **deck.gl/Kepler.gl** | GPU-accelerated rendering, smooth transitions | Geospatial focus | 46вҶ’ 47вҶ’### 2.2 Core Principles 48вҶ’ 49вҶ’1. **Density with Clarity** (Islands UI) - Pack information without overwhelming 50вҶ’2. **Dark Mode First** (Linear) - Reduce eye strain, modern aesthetic 51вҶ’3. **Modular Panels** (Fleet) - Visually separated functional areas 52вҶ’4. **Direct Manipulation** (Gantt) - Drag to reschedule, resize to extend 53вҶ’5. **GPU Performance** (deck.gl) - 60fps even with 15k+ tasks 54вҶ’ 55вҶ’--- 56вҶ’ 57вҶ’## 3. Moodboard 58вҶ’ 59вҶ’### 3.1 Aesthetic Reference: Linear App 60вҶ’ 61вҶ’The visual "mood" comes from Linear - minimalist, dark, typography-focused. 62вҶ’ 63вҶ’ 64вҶ’ 65вҶ’*Linear: Dark mode default, generous whitespace, bold typography* 66вҶ’ 67вҶ’ 68вҶ’ 69вҶ’*Linear: Multi-layered views with clean tab navigation* 70вҶ’ 71вҶ’### 3.2 Layout Reference: JetBrains Fleet Islands UI 72вҶ’ 73вҶ’Complex information organized into distinct "islands" - perfect for enterprise apps. 74вҶ’ 75вҶ’ 76вҶ’ 77вҶ’*Fleet Islands UI: Modular panels, transparent styling, reduced cognitive load* 78вҶ’ 79вҶ’ 80вҶ’ 81вҶ’*GitKraken: Similar density with clarity approach* 82вҶ’ 83вҶ’### 3.3 Domain Reference: Scheduling Software 84вҶ’ 85вҶ’Gantt chart patterns from scheduling software. 86вҶ’ 87вҶ’ 88вҶ’ 89вҶ’*DHTMLX Gantt: Dark theme, task bars with dependencies, timeline header* 90вҶ’ 91вҶ’### 3.4 Domain Reference: Railway Software 92вҶ’ 93вҶ’Fleet management patterns from railway industry. 94вҶ’ 95вҶ’ 96вҶ’ 97вҶ’*Railfleet: ECM fleet management, maintenance workflows* 98вҶ’ 99вҶ’### 3.5 Rendering Reference: deck.gl 100вҶ’ 101вҶ’GPU-accelerated visualization capabilities. 102вҶ’ 103вҶ’ 104вҶ’ 105вҶ’*Kepler.gl: deck.gl-powered data visualization* 106вҶ’ 107вҶ’ 108вҶ’ 109вҶ’*Flowmap.blue: Timeline-based flow visualization* 110вҶ’ 111вҶ’ 112вҶ’ 113вҶ’*SandDance: Interactive data exploration with smooth transitions* 114вҶ’ 115вҶ’--- 116вҶ’ 117вҶ’## 4. # MMS Design Specification > **Internal Document** - Implementation guide for the interactive demo with Vite + React 18 + deck.gl > > **Source of Truth**: `docs/RFQ.md` only > > **Primary Feature**: Planning View with Sandbox Mode (What-If Scenarios) --- ## 1. Executive Summary ### 1.1 Objective Win the bidding process by demonstrating an exceptional frontend solution that addresses all client pain points from the RFQ. The demo must visually impress while proving we can deliver a production-ready "Single Pane of Glass" for Rolling Stock Maintenance. ### 1.2 RFQ Pain Points вҶ’ Our Solutions | # | Client Pain Point (RFQ) | Our Solution | Key Visual Element | |---|------------------------|--------------|-------------------| | 1 | No dedicated mileage management tools | Purpose-built mileage dashboard | KPI Cards + Mileage indicators | | 2 | IBM Maximo only does asset management | Integrated mileage + maintenance planning | Unified Planning View | | 3 | Manual Excel management | Interactive visual timeline with drag operations | Gantt-style canvas | | 4 | Folder-driven data storage | Centralized fleet visualization | Sidebar + Fleet grid | | 5 | Limited maintenance-operations integration | Single pane for both teams | Shared workspace | | 6 | Non-compliance risk (underrun/overrun) | Visual alerts + compliance indicators | Color-coded tasks + banners | | 7 | **No predictive simulations** | **Sandbox Mode for what-if scenarios** | **PRIMARY FEATURE** | | 8 | Insufficient resource visibility | Resource utilization panels | Heatmap + capacity bars | | 9 | Manual handling delays | Automated scheduling visualization | Smart conflict detection | --- ## 2. Design Philosophy ### 2.1 Inspiration Attribution We draw from multiple sources, each contributing specific elements: | Source | What We Take | What We DON'T Take | |--------|--------------|-------------------| | **Linear App** | Dark mode aesthetic, typography, whitespace, micro-interactions | Sprint/issue tracking patterns (different domain) | | **JetBrains Fleet Islands UI** | Modular layout, panel separation, information density | IDE-specific features | | **DHTMLX Gantt** | Timeline visualization, task bars, dependency lines | Light theme default | | **Railnova Railfleet** | Fleet dashboard patterns, train status indicators | Specific ECM workflows | | **deck.gl/Kepler.gl** | GPU-accelerated rendering, smooth transitions | Geospatial focus | ### 2.2 Core Principles 1. **Density with Clarity** (Islands UI) - Pack information without overwhelming 2. **Dark Mode First** (Linear) - Reduce eye strain, modern aesthetic 3. **Modular Panels** (Fleet) - Visually separated functional areas 4. **Direct Manipulation** (Gantt) - Drag to reschedule, resize to extend 5. **GPU Performance** (deck.gl) - 60fps even with 15k+ tasks --- ## 3. Moodboard ### 3.1 Aesthetic Reference: Linear App The visual "mood" comes from Linear - minimalist, dark, typography-focused. *Linear: Dark mode default, generous whitespace, bold typography* *Linear: Multi-layered views with clean tab navigation* ### 3.2 Layout Reference: JetBrains Fleet Islands UI Complex information organized into distinct "islands" - perfect for enterprise apps. *Fleet Islands UI: Modular panels, transparent styling, reduced cognitive load* *GitKraken: Similar density with clarity approach* ### 3.3 Domain Reference: Scheduling Software Gantt chart patterns from scheduling software. *DHTMLX Gantt: Dark theme, task bars with dependencies, timeline header* ### 3.4 Domain Reference: Railway Software Fleet management patterns from railway industry. *Railfleet: ECM fleet management, maintenance workflows* ### 3.5 Rendering Reference: deck.gl GPU-accelerated visualization capabilities. *Kepler.gl: deck.gl-powered data visualization* *Flowmap.blue: Timeline-based flow visualization* *SandDance: Interactive data exploration with smooth transitions* --- ## 4. Design System ### 4.1 Color Palette (Dark Mode) ``` BACKGROUND (Islands UI inspired) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ bg-canvas: #0A0A0A (Main canvas background) bg-panel: #141414 (Island panels) bg-elevated: #1C1C1C (Cards, popovers) bg-hover: #262626 (Interactive hover) bg-active: #2A2A2A (Active/selected) BORDERS (Subtle separation) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ border-subtle: #1F1F1F (Panel edges) border-default: #2E2E2E (Dividers) border-strong: #404040 (Focus rings) TEXT (High contrast) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ text-primary: #FAFAFA (Headlines) text-secondary: #A1A1A1 (Body) text-tertiary: #6B6B6B (Labels) text-disabled: #404040 (Disabled) SEMANTIC - MAINTENANCE TYPES (From RFQ) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ a-check: #10B981 (Green - Quick, 10k km) b-check: #3B82F6 (Blue - Data Structures ### 9.1 Fleet Data (From RFQ) ```typescript // Fleet composition from RFQ const FLEET_CONFIG = { ALSTOM: { count: 79, prefix: 'ALT', depot: 'AL_RASHIDIYA' }, KINKI: { count: 49, prefix: 'KNK', depot: 'JEBEL_ALI' }, total: 128, }; // Contract period from RFQ const CONTRACT = { startYear: 2024, endYear: 2036, // 12 years }; // Maintenance intervals from RFQ const MAINTENANCE_TYPES = { 'A-Check': { intervalKm: 10_000, tolerance: 2_000, days: 1, manHours: 8 }, 'B-Check': { intervalKm: 50_000, tolerance: 5_000, days: 2, manHours: 32 }, 'C-Check': { intervalKm: 200_000, tolerance: 20_000, days: 3, manHours: 160 }, 'D-Overhaul': { intervalKm: 800_000, tolerance: 50_000, days: 20, manHours: 1200 }, }; ``` ### 9.2 Train Entity ```typescript interface Train { id: string; // "ALT-001" or "KNK-001" type: 'ALSTOM' | 'KINKI'; name: string; depot: 'AL_RASHIDIYA' | 'JEBEL_ALI'; currentMileageKm: number; avgDailyMileageKm: number; status: 'operational' | 'maintenance' | 'out-of-service'; lastMaintenanceDate: string; // ISO 8601 nextMaintenanceDue: string; // ISO 8601 } ``` ### 9.3 Task Entity ```typescript interface Task { id: string; trainId: string; startDate: string; // ISO 8601 endDate: string; // ISO 8601 type: 'A-Check' | 'B-Check' | 'C-Check' | 'D-Overhaul'; status: 'scheduled' | 'in-progress' | 'completed' | 'cancelled'; manHours: number; track: number; // 1-5 hasConflict: boolean; conflictReason?: string; dependencies?: string[]; // Task IDs estimatedCost: number; } ``` ### 9.4 Scenario Entity (Sandbox) ```typescript interface Scenario { id: string; name: string; description?: string; createdAt: string; modifiedAt: string; changes: ScenarioChange[]; metrics: { opexDelta: number; // vs baseline overrunRiskCount: number; trackUtilizationDelta: number; }; } interface ScenarioChange { type: 'move' | 'resize' | 'create' | 'delete' | 'modify'; taskId: string; before: Partial | null; after: Partial | null; timestamp: string; } ``` --- ## 10. Interactions & Gestures ### 10.1 Canvas Navigation | Input | Action | Handler | |-------|--------|---------| | Drag | Pan canvas | @use-gesture onDrag | | Scroll wheel | Zoom (cursor-centered) | @use-gesture onWheel | | Pinch (touch) | Zoom | @use-gesture onPinch | | Double-click | Zoom in to point | Custom handler | | Double-tap (touch) | Zoom in to point | Custom handler | ### 10.2 Task Interactions | Input | Live Mode | Sandbox Mode | |-------|-----------|--------------| | Hover | Tooltip | Tooltip + resize handles | | Click | Select task | Select task | | Drag task | - | Move (reschedule) | | Drag edge | - | Resize duration | | Right-click | View menu | Edit menu | | Delete key | - | Delete selected | ### 10.3 Keyboard Shortcuts | Key | Action | |-----|--------| | `+` / `=` | Zoom in | | `-` | Zoom out | | `0` | Reset zoom to 1x | | Arrow keys | Pan canvas | | `Escape` | Clear selection | | `Delete` | Delete task (Sandbox) | | `Ctrl+Z` | Undo (Sandbox) | | `Ctrl+Shift+Z` | Redo (Sandbox) | | `S` | Toggle Sandbox mode | --- ## 11. All button variants в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ Card.stories.tsx # Panel cards в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ Input.stories.tsx # Search, text inputs в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ Toggle.stories.tsx # Mode toggle (LIVE/SANDBOX) в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ Badge.stories.tsx # Warning/danger alerts в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ TrainRow.stories.tsx # Fleet list item в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җ TaskBlock.stories.tsx # Planning task (all states) в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ canvas/ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ MacroLayer.stories.tsx # Organization:** ``` stories/ в”ңв”Җв”Җ 01-wireframes/ # Phase 1: Low-fidelity в”ңв”Җв”Җ 02-layout/ # Phase 2: Design system applied в”ңв”Җв”Җ 03-components/ # Phase 3: Interactive components в”Ӯ quality gates before proceeding: | Phase | Gate | Criteria | |-------|------|----------| | 1 | **Layout Approval** | Stakeholder sign-off on information architecture | | 2 | **Design Approval** | Visual design meets enterprise standards | | 3 | **Functionality Approval** | All interactions work correctly | | 4 | **Release Approval** | Performance, accessibility, polish complete | **Enterprise Quality Checklist:** ``` VISUAL QUALITY (Not "Amateur") в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ в–Ў в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ в–Ў Hover states on all interactive elements в–Ў Focus indicators visible (keyboard nav) в–Ў Click targets minimum 44Г—44px в–Ў Tooltips appear on hover (300ms delay) в–Ў Drag operations have visual feedback в–Ў Undo is always available in Sandbox PERFORMANCE QUALITY в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ в–Ў Initial load < 3s в–Ў 60fps during pan/zoom в–Ў No jank during LOD transitions в–Ў Viewport culling active (only visible rendered) в–Ў Memory stable over 5min session POLISH Transitions are 200-300ms, easing applied в–Ў Cursors indicate interaction affordance в–Ў Favicon and page title set в–Ў Console has no warnings/errors ``` --- *Document Version: 2.1* *Created:      5вҶ’
     6вҶ’## Executive Summary
     7вҶ’

     9вҶ’
    10вҶ’1. **Viewport Culling** - Only render/process entities visible in current viewport

    14вҶ’
    15вҶ’**Key Finding from PDF Research:** The primary bottleneck in WebGL applications on mobile is **Garbage Collection**, not rendering. Traditional React patterns (useState, Proxy-based stores) are inadequate for 60fps updates.
    16вҶ’

    18вҶ’

    33вҶ’## Current State Analysis
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’### What We Have
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’| Component | Current Implementation | Limitation |
    38вҶ’|-----------|----------------------|------------|
    39вҶ’| **MesoLayer** | InstancedMesh with 60k instances | No per-instance culling - all instances sent to GPU |
    40вҶ’| **MacroLayer** | InstancedMesh heatmap cells | Same limitation |
    41вҶ’| **MicroLayer** | HTML overlay + frustum filter | Has `filterVisibleTasks()` but iterates O(n) |
    42вҶ’| **Data Storage** | JavaScript objects (AoS pattern) | GC pressure, poor cache locality |
    43вҶ’| **Spatial Queries** | Linear search O(n) | Slow viewport queries |
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’### The Problem
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’```
    48вҶ’Current Flow:
    49вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

    51вҶ’в”Ӯ  tasks      в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  to GPU     в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  fragments  в”Ӯ
    52вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
    53вҶ’
    54вҶ’Even if only 100 tasks are visible, we:
    55вҶ’1. Iterate 60k tasks in JavaScript (CPU)
    56вҶ’2. Upload 60k instances to GPU (bandwidth)
    57вҶ’3. GPU processes all vertex shaders (GPU compute)
    58вҶ’4. GPU only then culls fragments (wasted work)

    66вҶ’в”Ӯ  Viewport   в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ  R-tree     в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ  ~500 tasks в”Ӯ
    67вҶ’в”Ӯ  bounds     в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  query O(log n)в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  to GPU     в”Ӯ
    68вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
    69вҶ’
    70вҶ’With viewport culling:
    71вҶ’1. Query R-tree for visible tasks (~4ms for 1000 results)
    72вҶ’2. Update only visible instances
    73вҶ’3. Set InstancedMesh.count to visible count
    74вҶ’4. Minimal GPU work
    75вҶ’```
    76вҶ’
    77вҶ’---
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’## Three.js InstancedMesh Culling
    80вҶ’
    81вҶ’### Native Three.js Limitation
    82вҶ’
    83вҶ’From [Three.js Forum Discussion](https://discourse.threejs.org/t/how-to-do-frustum-culling-with-instancedmesh/22633):
    84вҶ’
    85вҶ’> "In native object mode, meshes are removed by the view frustum culling step in the Three.js render pipeline and never sent to the GPU. In instanced mode, however, all the meshes are sent to the GPU."
    86вҶ’
    87вҶ’### Solution 1: InstancedMesh2 Library
    88вҶ’
    89вҶ’The [@three.ez/instanced-mesh](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@three.ez/instanced-mesh) library provides:
    90вҶ’
    91вҶ’- **Per-instance frustum culling** - skip rendering for out-of-view instances
    92вҶ’- **Object3D-like instances** - use instances like Object3D with transforms
    93вҶ’- **Dynamic capacity** - add or remove instances seamlessly
    94вҶ’- **BVH spatial indexing** - O(log n) frustum culling

    99вҶ’
   100вҶ’const mesh = new InstancedMesh2(geometry, material, {

# Viewport Culling & Massive Data Rendering Research

> Research document for MMS Canvas optimization
> Last updated: 2025-12-17

## Executive Summary

This document consolidates research on best practices for rendering massive datasets in WebGL, with specific focus on:

1. **Viewport Culling** - Only render/process entities visible in current viewport
2. **Spatial Indexing** - O(log n) queries instead of O(n) iteration
3. **Memory Management** - Eliminate GC pauses via TypedArrays and Object Pooling
4. **Draw Call Optimization** - Minimize GPU state changes

**Key Finding from PDF Research:** The primary bottleneck in WebGL applications on mobile is **Garbage Collection**, not rendering. Traditional React patterns (useState, Proxy-based stores) are inadequate for 60fps updates.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Current State Analysis](#current-state-analysis)
2. [Three.js InstancedMesh Culling](#threejs-instancedmesh-culling)
3. [Spatial Indexing with RBush](#spatial-indexing-with-rbush)
4. [Deck.gl Techniques](#deckgl-techniques)
5. [Figma Architecture Insights](#figma-architecture-insights)
6. [Memory Management](#memory-management)
7. [Proposed Architecture](#proposed-architecture)
8. [Implementation Phases](#implementation-phases)
9. [References](#references)

---

## Current State Analysis

### What We Have

| Component | Current Implementation | Limitation |
|-----------|----------------------|------------|
| **MesoLayer** | InstancedMesh with 60k instances | No per-instance culling - all instances sent to GPU |
| **MacroLayer** | InstancedMesh heatmap cells | Same limitation |
| **MicroLayer** | HTML overlay + frustum filter | Has `filterVisibleTasks()` but iterates O(n) |
| **Data Storage** | JavaScript objects (AoS pattern) | GC pressure, poor cache locality |
| **Spatial Queries** | Linear search O(n) | Slow viewport queries |

### The Problem

```
Current Flow:
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  All 60k    в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ  All sent   в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ  GPU culls  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  tasks      в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  to GPU     в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  fragments  в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

Even if only 100 tasks are visible, we:
1. Iterate 60k tasks in JavaScript (CPU)
2. Upload 60k instances to GPU (bandwidth)
3. GPU processes all vertex shaders (GPU compute)
4. GPU only then culls fragments (wasted work)
```

### Target State

```
Optimized Flow:
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  Viewport   в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ  R-tree     в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ  ~500 tasks в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  bounds     в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  query O(log n)в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  to GPU     в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

With viewport culling:
1. Query R-tree for visible tasks (~4ms for 1000 results)
2. Update only visible instances
3. Set InstancedMesh.count to visible count
4. Minimal GPU work
```

---

## Three.js InstancedMesh Culling

### Native Three.js Limitation

From [Three.js Forum Discussion](https://discourse.threejs.org/t/how-to-do-frustum-culling-with-instancedmesh/22633):

> "In native object mode, meshes are removed by the view frustum culling step in the Three.js render pipeline and never sent to the GPU. In instanced mode, however, all the meshes are sent to the GPU."

### Solution 1: InstancedMesh2 Library

The [@three.ez/instanced-mesh](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@three.ez/instanced-mesh) library provides:

- **Per-instance frustum culling** - skip rendering for out-of-view instances
- **Object3D-like instances** - use instances like Object3D with transforms
- **Dynamic capacity** - add or remove instances seamlessly
- **BVH spatial indexing** - O(log n) frustum culling

const mesh = new InstancedMesh2(geometry, material, {

# UX Research: Canvas Pan/Zoom Best Practices

> Research conducted for UX-AUDIT-001 to inform F118-F121 implementation.

---

## 1. Industry Leader Patterns

### 1.1 Figma

| Gesture | Action | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Scroll wheel | Pan vertically | Default behavior (browser compatibility) |
| Ctrl/Cmd + Scroll | Zoom in/out | Standard modifier pattern |
| Middle-click drag | Pan in any direction | Alternative pan gesture |
| Two-finger trackpad | Pan (both axes) | Natural for laptop users |
| Pinch trackpad | Zoom | Native gesture support |
| Spacebar + drag | Pan | Classic design tool pattern |
| Z key + click | Zoom to point | Precision zoom gesture |

**Key Insight:** Figma made a deliberate compromise - scroll wheel pans vertically by default (browser-native behavior) rather than zooming. This prevents scroll hijacking on pages with embedded Figma content.

**Customization:** Users can enable "Scroll wheel zoom" in Preferences, making scroll = zoom (without Ctrl).

### 1.2 Google Maps

| Gesture | Action | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Drag | Pan with momentum | Velocity-based inertia |
| Scroll wheel | Zoom | Centers on cursor |
| Double-click | Zoom in 2x | At click location |
| Pinch | Zoom | Touch devices |
| Two-finger drag | Pan | Touch devices |

**Gesture Handling Modes:**
- `cooperative`: Scroll pans page; Ctrl+scroll zooms map
- `greedy`: Scroll always zooms map
- `none`: Disable interactions

**Camera Easing:** Google uses `Quadratic.Out` easing with tween.js for smooth camera transitions. Typical duration: 300-500ms for zoom, 15s for animated tours.

**Momentum Physics:**
- Pan has momentum/inertia after release
- Zoom does NOT have momentum (intentional UX decision)
- Friction coefficient creates natural deceleration

### 1.3 Apple Maps / iOS

| Gesture | Action | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| One-finger drag | Pan with strong momentum | "Buttery" feel |
| Pinch | Zoom with momentum | Unlike Google Maps |
| Double-tap | Zoom in | Fixed 2x increment |
| Two-finger tap | Zoom out | Fixed 0.5x increment |
| Rotate gesture | Rotate map | Optional feature |

**Key Differentiator:** iOS implements momentum for BOTH pan AND zoom gestures, creating a more fluid feel than Google Maps.

### 1.4 Canva

| Gesture | Action | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Drag | Pan canvas | Infinite canvas |
| Scroll wheel | Zoom | Centers on cursor |
| Pinch | Zoom | Touch devices |
| Pan gesture | Pan | Two-finger drag |

**Infinite Canvas:** Canva's whiteboard feature supports unlimited pan in all directions with zoom/pan/scroll navigation.

---

## 2. Mouse Events Reference

### 2.1 Event Types

| Event | Triggers | Properties |
|-------|----------|------------|
| `mousedown` | Button pressed | `button`, `buttons`, `clientX/Y` |
| `mouseup` | Button released | `button`, `buttons`, `clientX/Y` |
| `mousemove` | Cursor moved | `clientX/Y`, `movementX/Y` |
| `click` | Press + release (same element) | Fires after `mouseup` |
| `dblclick` | Two clicks within threshold | ~300ms default |
| `contextmenu` | Right-click | Prevent for custom menu |
| `wheel` | Scroll wheel rotated | `deltaX/Y/Z`, `deltaMode` |
| `mouseenter` | Cursor enters element | Does NOT bubble |
| `mouseleave` | Cursor leaves element | Does NOT bubble |
| `mouseover` | Cursor over element | Bubbles (includes children) |
| `mouseout` | Cursor leaves element | Bubbles (includes children) |

### 2.2 Button Values

| `event.button` | Description |
|----------------|-------------|
| 0 | Left button (primary) |
| 1 | Middle button (wheel click) |
| 2 | Right button (secondary) |
| 3 | Back button (browser) |
| 4 | Forward button (browser) |

### 2.3 Drag Implementation Pattern

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive view of all the design files. Let me create a detailed summary report of the design specifications and identify any issues or inconsistencies.

## DESIGN SPECIFICATION ANALYSIS REPORT

I've thoroughly explored all design reference files in your MFC project. Here's a comprehensive analysis of the design requirements, potential issues, and implementation considerations:

---

### FILE INVENTORY

**Core Design Documents:**
1. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC/docs/MMS_STYLE_GUIDE.md` - Comprehensive style guide with moodboard, design system, and tech stack
2. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC/docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md` - Detailed implementation guide for Vite + React + deck.gl demo
3. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC/docs/design/README.md` - Vision document and screen reference analysis
4. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC/docs/design/UX_SPECIFICATION.md` - Infinite canvas UX for railway maintenance planning
5. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC/docs/design/UX_RESEARCH.md` - Canvas pan/zoom best practices (partial)
6. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC/docs/design/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` - Viewport culling and massive data rendering (partial)

**Visual Assets:**
- 7 reference images (screen-1 through screen-7.png)
- Multiple moodboard images from industry leaders (DHTMLX, deck.gl, Grafana, Linear, etc.)

---

### CRITICAL DESIGN INCONSISTENCIES & ISSUES

#### **1. CONFLICTING COLOR PALETTES (MAJOR ISSUE)**

The documents define **three different color systems**, which is problematic:

| Document | Background | Primary Text | Task Colors |
|----------|-----------|--------------|-------------|
| **MMS_STYLE_GUIDE.md** (Section 4.1) | `#0f172a` (slate-900) | `#f1f5f9` (slate-100) | Blue/Violet/Pink/Orange |
| **DESIGN_SPEC.md** (Section 4.1) | `#0A0A0A` (pure black) | `#FAFAFA` (almost white) | A-Check=Green/B=Blue/C=Amber/D=Red |
| **UX_SPECIFICATION.md** (Section 7.1) | `#f4f4f5` (zinc-100 LIGHT) | Dark text (implicit) | Blue/Amber/Gray |

**Issue:** UX_SPECIFICATION describes a LIGHT theme, while other documents mandate DARK mode.

**Recommendation:** 
- Choose ONE official palette
- MMS_STYLE_GUIDE (slate-900) is most professional and matches moodboard (voestalpine, Grafana)
- Create light mode as explicitly labeled variant, not default

---

#### **2. TASK/MAINTENANCE TYPE COLOR MAPPING CONFLICT**

**MMS_STYLE_GUIDE.md:**
```
PM1 = #60a5fa (blue-400)
PM2 = #a78bfa (violet-400)
PM3 = #f472b6 (pink-400)
Overhaul = #fb923c (orange-400)
```

**DESIGN_SPEC.md:**
```
A-Check = #10B981 (green)
B-Check = #3B82F6 (blue)
C-Check = #F59E0B (amber)
D-Overhaul = #EF4444 (red)
```

These are **completely different systems** with different color meanings!

**Critical Question:** Are PM1/PM2/PM3 and A-Check/B-Check/C-Check the same thing?
- If YES: Need unified terminology and colors
- If NO: Need documentation explaining the distinction

---

#### **3. TYPOGRAPHY SCALE INCONSISTENCY**

**MMS_STYLE_GUIDE.md:**
- h1: 24px / 600 / -0.02em
- h2: 18px / 600 / -0.01em
- h3: 15px / 600
- body: 13px / 400
- caption: 11px / 500

**DESIGN_SPEC.md:**
- h1: 24px / 600
- h2: 18px / 600
- h3: 15px / 600
- body: 13px / 400
- caption: 11px / 500

**Difference:** Letter-spacing values omitted in DESIGN_SPEC (are they inherited or not applied?)

---

#### **4. SPACING/GRID SYSTEM ISSUE**

**MMS_STYLE_GUIDE.md (Section 4.3):**
```
xs: 4px
sm: 8px
md: 12px
lg: 16px
xl: 24px
2xl: 32px
```

**DESIGN_SPEC.md (Section 4.3):**
```
xs: 4px
sm: 8px
md: 12px
lg: 16px
xl: 24px
2xl: 32px
```

вң… **These match** - Good!

---

#### **5. LAYOUT CONTRADICTION - 2D vs ISLANDS UI**

**MMS_STYLE_GUIDE.md claims:**
- "Islands UI Pattern" (section 5.1 in DESIGN_SPEC)
- Modular, separated panels
- "Dark Mode First"

**But the actual layout diagrams show:**
- Sidebar (160px) left-aligned
- Canvas area spanning right (standard 2-column)
- Status bar bottom

**This is NOT Islands UI** (which would show floating, scattered panels). It's a standard Navbar + Sidebar + Canvas layout.

**Issue:** Marketing language vs actual design don't match. The term "Islands UI" is misleading if not actually implemented.

---

#### **6. SEMANTIC ZOOM LEVEL THRESHOLDS MISMATCH**

**MMS_STYLE_GUIDE.md (Section 3.1):**
```
Strategic (< 0.3x)    | 10-12 years
Tactical (0.3-1.0x)  | 1-3 years
Operational (1-3x)   | 1-6 months
Execution (> 3x)     | 1-4 weeks
```

**DESIGN_SPEC.md (Section 6.1):**
```
MACRO (< 0.5x)   | 10-12 years
MESO (0.5-2.0x)  | 1-3 years
MICRO (> 2.0x)   | 1-8 weeks
```

**UX_SPECIFICATION.md (Section 4.1):**
```
MACRO (< 0.5x)   | 10-20 years
MESO (0.5-2.0x)  | 2-5 years
MICRO (> 2.0x)   | 4-8 weeks
```

**Critical issues:**
- 3 vs 4 LOD levels
- Threshold boundaries differ (0.3x vs 0.5x)
- Time spans differ (which is authoritative?)

**Recommendation:** DESIGN_SPEC.md (3 levels) is more implementable. Lock that down.

---

#### **7. COMPONENT HEIGHT SPECIFICATIONS MISSING**

**MMS_STYLE_GUIDE.md (Section 4.3):**
> "Height: 24px (operational), 16px (tactical), 8px (strategic)"

But **where is this applied?** Is this:
- Task block heights in canvas?
- Row heights in sidebar?
- Icon sizes?

No reference in other documents.

---

#### **8. SANDBOX MODE COLORS NOT IN MAIN PALETTE**

**DESIGN_SPEC.md (Section 4.1):**
```
sandbox-accent: #8B5CF6 (Violet-500)
sandbox-bg: #0F0A1A (Violet-tinted canvas)
sandbox-border: #6D28D9 (Violet-700)
```

These are **not referenced in MMS_STYLE_GUIDE** at all. Are these the official secondary palette, or ad-hoc additions?

**Recommendation:** Document sandbox colors in the main style guide.

---

#### **9. MINIMAP SPECIFICATION INCOMPLETE**

**MMS_STYLE_GUIDE.md (Section 4.3):**
> "Size: 120px Г— 60px, Position: Bottom-right corner (fixed)"

**But:**
- What coordinates relative to canvas?
- Padding from edge?
- Handles drag interaction - what's the drag feedback?
- How does minimap styling work (colors, borders)?

**DESIGN_SPEC.md** doesn't expand on this.

---

#### **10. STATE MANAGEMENT PHILOSOPHY CONTRADICTION**

**MMS_STYLE_GUIDE.md (Section 5):**
> "Prohibited Patterns: вқҢ useState, вқҢ useReducer, вқҢ useEffect"
> "Allowed: useRef, useSyncExternalStore, useMemo"

**DESIGN_SPEC.md Section 12** recommends:
> "XState 5.x - Viewport & sandbox state machines"

**This is consistent** вң… BUT:

**Neither document addresses:**
- How to integrate deck.gl (which has its own state management)
- How to handle React lifecycle events without useEffect
- How animations work without useState for interpolated values

---

#### **11. ANIMATION DURATION SPECIFICATIONS INCOMPLETE**

**MMS_STYLE_GUIDE.md (Section 4.4):**
```
--duration-fast: 100ms
--duration-normal: 200ms
--duration-slow: 300ms
--duration-zoom: 400ms
```

**But:**
- What easing for each? (only --ease-out-expo and --ease-out-cubic listed)
- Mapping to actions unclear (which should use 100ms vs 200ms?)

**DESIGN_SPEC.md (Section 7):**
> "LOD Transition: Opacity crossfade using Hermite smoothstep, Duration: 200ms, Easing: easeOutExpo"

**Good detail here** вң… but not comprehensive.

---

#### **12. BROWSER SUPPORT MISMATCH**

**UX_SPECIFICATION.md (Section 13):**
> "Safari 15+ - Full, Mobile Safari - Partial (Touch gestures only)"

**But MMS_STYLE_GUIDE.md** emphasizes:
> "External state management only (no useState/useReducer)"
> "Mature libraries only (8+ years of evolution)"

**Question:** Does "Mature libraries" include polyfills for older browsers? Or are we dropping IE11/older Safari entirely?

---

### DESIGN REQUIREMENTS SUMMARY (CONSOLIDATED)

#### **Color Palette (Official - MMS_STYLE_GUIDE.md)**

**Background:**
- `--bg-primary: #0f172a` (main canvas)
- `--bg-secondary: #1e293b` (cards)
- `--bg-tertiary: #334155` (hover)

**Text:**
- `--text-primary: #f1f5f9` (headlines)
- `--text-secondary: #94a3b8` (body)
- `--text-muted: #64748b` (labels)

**Maintenance Types:**
- PM1: `#60a5fa` (light inspection)
- PM2: `#a78bfa` (medium)
- PM3: `#f472b6` (heavy)
- Overhaul: `#fb923c` (major)

**Semantic:**
- Healthy: `#22c55e` (green)
- Warning: `#f59e0b` (amber)
- Critical: `#ef4444` (red)
- Info: `#3b82f6` (blue)

---

#### **Typography Stack**

**Fonts:**
- Primary: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Segoe UI, sans-serif
- Monospace: JetBrains Mono, Fira Code, Consolas

**Scale:**
- xs (11px) - Labels on Gantt bars
- sm (13px) - Secondary info
- base (14px) - Body text
- lg (16px) - Section headers
- xl (20px) - Panel titles
- 2xl (24px) - Page titles
- mono (12px) - Train IDs

---

#### **Spacing Grid (4px base)**
- xs: 4px
- sm: 8px
- md: 12px
- lg: 16px
- xl: 24px
- 2xl: 32px

---

#### **Border Radius**
- sm: 4px (buttons, inputs)
- md: 6px (cards, panels)
- lg: 8px (modals)
- full: 9999px (pills, badges)

---

#### **Semantic Zoom Levels (FINAL)**

| LOD | Zoom | Time Span | Rendering | Purpose |
|-----|------|-----------|-----------|---------|
| **Strategic** | < 0.5x | 10-12 years | Heatmap (deck.gl) | Capital planning |
| **Tactical** | 0.5-2.0x | 1-3 years | Task blocks (instanced) | Workload balancing |
| **Operational** | > 2.0x | 1-8 weeks | Detailed cards (SVG/HTML) | Task scheduling |

---

#### **Layout Structure**

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ NAVBAR [48px] - Zoom controls, mode toggle       в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ SIDEBAR    в”Ӯ TIMELINE HEADER                     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ [160px]    в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
